title: Phelipanche trichocalyx
taxonomy: Phelipanche trichocalyx is classified within the family Orobanchaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as broomrapes or dodders. Phelipanche trichocalyx is a type of holoparasitic plant, meaning it obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. The Orobanchaceae family includes approximately 200 species, with Phelipanche trichocalyx being one of the species within this group. Phelipanche trichocalyx is native to the Mediterranean region.

intro: Phelipanche trichocalyx, also known as Broomrape, is a parasitic plant species that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. Traditionally, Phelipanche trichocalyx has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Phelipanche trichocalyx include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Phelipanche trichocalyx is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or dried extract, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to treat respiratory conditions and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
